title: Vantive PrisMax CRRT - Blood Leak Detector Alarm
description: Guide for troubleshooting blood leak detector alarms on the Vantive PrisMax CRRT system, focusing on external causes before internal sensor issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Pause the therapy and isolate the patient from the affected circuit if safe. Move the patient to an alternative CRRT machine or ensure vascular access is secured. Why: Prevents patient exposure to potential blood leakage into the effluent line.

steps.title: Inspect the Blood Leak Detector Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Check for visible blood, condensation, or fluid on the detector window or optical sensors. Clean the sensor using manufacturer-approved wipes or procedures. Why: Dirt, water droplets, or condensation can trigger false alarms.

steps.title: Check Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure all bloodlines are properly seated, clamped, and routed correctly through the detector. Verify that the sensor slot is free of kinks or tubing misalignment. Why: Improperly positioned tubing can interrupt optical detection, causing an alarm.

steps.title: Inspect for Leaks or Damaged Bloodlines
steps.instructions: Examine arterial and venous lines for cracks, loose connectors, or leaks. Replace any compromised lines before restarting therapy. Why: Real blood leaks must be corrected immediately to prevent patient harm.

steps.title: Reset the Machine Alarm
steps.instructions: After inspection and correction, reset the alarm and re-prime the circuit if necessary. Observe the detector to ensure no alarm recurs.

steps.title: Verify Consumables and Expiry
steps.instructions: Confirm the dialyzer, tubing set, and detector sensors are within their usage limits. Replace any expired or previously flagged consumables.

CCR.Complaint: Blood leak detector alarm triggered during therapy.
CCR.Cause: Sensor contaminated with condensation and tubing slightly misaligned in detector slot.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned sensor window, repositioned bloodline, reset alarm, and therapy resumed without further alarms.

title: Vantive PrisMax CRRT - Effluent Scale Error
description: Guide to troubleshoot effluent scale errors on the Vantive PrisMax CRRT system.

steps.title: Ensure the machine is on a level, stable surface
steps.instructions: A tilted or unstable platform can cause the effluent scale to misread weight. Level the machine using adjustable feet if available.

steps.title: Check for obstruction under the effluent bag
steps.instructions: Remove any packaging, tubing, or debris that might contact the scale. Ensure nothing is pressing against the bag that could affect weight readings.

steps.title: Verify effluent bag placement
steps.instructions: Confirm the bag is properly seated in the scale basket or holder. Misaligned bags can trigger a scale error even if the bag is empty.

steps.title: Inspect the scale mechanism and load cells visually
steps.instructions: Look for visible damage, corrosion, or foreign objects under or around the scale. Gently clean surfaces if necessary using manufacturer-approved cleaning methods.

steps.title: Restart the CRRT system
steps.instructions: Power down and restart the machine to reset scale calibration errors. Observe if the error clears during startup.

steps.title: Check software prompts for recalibration
steps.instructions: If the system prompts for scale calibration, follow on-screen instructions. Ensure calibration is performed with the effluent bag empty and properly positioned.

CCR.Complaint: “Effluent scale error” alarm during therapy setup.
CCR.Cause: Misaligned effluent bag, slight debris under scale, or level inconsistency affecting weight measurement.
CCR.Resolution: Level machine, remove obstruction, correctly reposition bag, restart device, and perform recalibration. If error persisted, device removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Error cleared or persisted after system restart and calibration.
Final device status: back in service or removed for repair.

title: Vantive PrisMax CRRT - Air Detector Alarm
description: Guide for troubleshooting Vantive PrisMax CRRT air detector alarms, focusing on external causes before internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop therapy and clamp lines if safe. Switch patient to alternate CRRT device or therapy line if necessary. Air alarms must be cleared before resuming therapy.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing for Air
steps.instructions: Check all bloodlines for visible air bubbles. Ensure all connections are fully seated and tight. Remove any bubbles using the manufacturer-recommended procedure (e.g., prime or manual air removal).

steps.title: Check Blood Flow and Circuit Position
steps.instructions: Verify that the circuit is positioned correctly in the machine. Ensure the tubing is not kinked or looped in a way that traps air. Confirm correct connection to arterial and venous ports.

steps.title: Examine the Air Detector Sensor
steps.instructions: Inspect the air detector chamber for contamination, moisture, or residue. Clean the chamber according to external cleaning guidelines—do not disassemble internal electronics. Ensure sensor is seated properly in its holder.

steps.title: Confirm Machine Setup
steps.instructions: Verify the correct therapy prescription is loaded. Ensure alarms are acknowledged and the system is in ready mode. Check that the priming procedure was performed correctly.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn off the machine, wait 30 seconds, and power back on. Recheck for air alarm clearance before resuming therapy.

CCR.Complaint: CRRT machine alarmed for air in line, therapy paused unexpectedly.
CCR.Cause: Visible air bubble detected in venous line; air detector chamber appeared misaligned and slightly contaminated.
CCR.Resolution: Circuit was cleared of air, air detector chamber cleaned externally, connections verified, therapy restarted successfully.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior before and after troubleshooting
Final device status: Ready for use / Out of Service

title: Vantive PrisMax CRRT - Pump Module Failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for pump module failure on the Vantive PrisMax CRRT system, focusing on external checks before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm that the patient is safely transitioned to another CRRT device if therapy must continue. Never troubleshoot a pump module while the patient is actively connected.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify AC power or battery status. Ensure the device is plugged into a known working outlet. Inspect power cables for damage.

steps.title: Inspect Pump Cassette and Tubing
steps.instructions: Open the pump module compartment and check the cassette is seated correctly. Ensure all tubing lines are free of kinks, clots, or air. Confirm that tubing connections match the intended setup per manufacturer guidelines.

steps.title: Reset the Pump Module
steps.instructions: Power cycle the device following standard shutdown/startup procedures. Observe if the pump error persists upon reboot.

steps.title: Examine the Pump Mechanism
steps.instructions: Look for visible obstructions or residue in the pump rollers or chamber. Ensure the cassette locks and roller mechanisms move freely.

steps.title: Check Device Software and Error Logs
steps.instructions: Review displayed error codes and log details to confirm pump failure. Record error codes for potential vendor support if escalation is needed.

CCR.Complaint: User reported the PrisMax alarmed and displayed a “Pump Module Failure” message during therapy.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering observed correct cassette installation and tubing, confirmed power and connections, but pump error persisted, indicating internal module failure.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer repair. Backup CRRT initiated for patient therapy continuity.

helpfulDetails: Device labeled and removed from service

title: Vantive PrisMax CRRT - Cartridge Not Recognized
description: Troubleshooting guide for situations where the Vantive PrisMax CRRT system fails to recognize a cartridge.

steps.title: Verify Cartridge Type
steps.instructions: Confirm that the cartridge is compatible with the PrisMax system. Using the wrong cartridge may prevent recognition.

steps.title: Check Cartridge Orientation
steps.instructions: Remove the cartridge and reinstall according to the labeling. Misalignment can prevent sensors from detecting the cartridge properly.

steps.title: Inspect Cartridge Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Ensure the barcode and sensor contacts are clean, free of debris, or condensation. Wipe gently with a lint-free cloth if needed.

steps.title: Check Machine Prompts
steps.instructions: Ensure the machine is prompting for the correct cartridge slot. Some alarms occur if the system expects the cartridge in a different slot.

steps.title: Verify Disposable Set Installation
steps.instructions: Confirm all tubing and lines are correctly seated and clamped. Loose connections can sometimes prevent cartridge detection.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn off the PrisMax, wait 30 seconds, and turn back on. This can reset sensor communication errors.

steps.title: Check for Software Updates / Error Codes
steps.instructions: Review the event log for any system-specific messages. Record error codes for documentation.

CCR.Complaint: Cartridge not recognized” when attempting to start therapy.
CCR.Cause: Cartridge misaligned and sensor contacts dirty, preventing detection.
CCR.Resolution: Reinstalled cartridge correctly, cleaned sensor contacts, device recognized cartridge. If unresolved, device labeled Out of Service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Verify outlet power and system battery status.
Check all tubing and clamps are properly seated.
Record any unusual alarms, beeps, or display messages.
Document cartridge lot numbers for traceability.
Final device status after troubleshooting (ready for use or Out of Service).
